commands.review_summary:
The PR bumps `@microsoft/mxc-sdk` to 0.7.0, adds explicit MXC support gates for Windows build 26200.8728+, changes sandboxed `system.run` shell/env/fallback behavior, updates Sandbox UI status text, and expands MXC/system.run tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main still gates MXC to Windows build 26300.8289+ and SDK 0.6.1, while this PR adds the 26200.8728+ path. I did not run Windows 26200 runtime proof in this read-only Linux checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainer review is needed for the sandbox UI-boundary policy, fail-closed upgrade behavior, and MXC PR sequencing; this is not a safe automated repair lane.

target.title: Validate MXC SDK 0.7.0 on Windows build 26200.8728
target.body:
Tracking issue: #784
Depends on: #785

Contributor-fork note: this branch is stacked on the PR1 commit, but the PR targets `main` because the PR1 base branch exists in the contributor fork, not in the upstream repository. After PR1 is merged or maintainers provide a preferred upstream base branch, this PR can be rebased/retargeted so the review diff only contains the MXC changes.

## Summary

This PR updates the Windows MXC dependency and availability gate for explicit build/UBR validation:

- Update `@microsoft/mxc-sdk` from `0.6.1` to `0.7.0`.
- Replace the single hardcoded supported Windows build with an explicit temporary processcontainer support table.
- Add processcontainer support for Windows build `26200.8728+`.
- Preserve processcontainer support for Windows build `26300.8289+`.
- Keep `isolation_session` separately gated to `26300.8553+`, matching SDK `0.7.0` documentation/source.
- Emit MXC config schema `0.7.0-alpha` for SDK `0.7.0`.
- Keep AppContainer-DACL fallback compatible by avoiding protected PATH-directory grants, omitting nonexistent denied-path roots, and granting only backend-safe PATH directories.
- Reject custom `system.run` environment variables in MXC mode until the Windows MXC `0.7.0` processcontainer backend supports non-empty `process.env`.
- Bootstrap `TEMP`, `TMP`, `TMPDIR`, and `PATH` inside the launched shell because non-empty MXC `process.env` is not usable on this backend.
- Fail closed when sandboxing is enabled but MXC is unavailable or disappears at runtime; host execution requires the operator to turn the sandbox off explicitly.
- Keep the MXC default shell on Windows PowerShell 5.1 for this AppContainer-DACL tier, while normal host execution prefers PowerShell 7 (`pwsh`) when available and falls back to Windows PowerShell only if `pwsh` is missing.
- Update Sandbox page status text so unavailable MXC means blocked sandboxed commands, not silent uncontained execution.
- Add unit coverage for accepted/rejected processcontainer and `isolation_session` build/UBR combinations.

This is intended as PR2 in the Windows 25H2 / MXC validation tracking issue. The tracking issue should remain open for PR3, which will cover full Gateway-mode and installed Companion validation after that broader path is tested safely.

## Why

The local machine is Windows Release Preview 25H2 build `26200.8728`. The MXC SDK probe can report usable processcontainer/AppContainer-DACL support locally, but OpenClaw's current availability gate rejects the machine because it only allows build `26300`.

This PR keeps the gate explicit and narrow. It does not add a broad `>= 26200` rule and does not enable `isolation_session` on `26200`.

## Changed Files

- `package.json`
- `package-lock.json`
- `src/OpenClaw.Shared/Capabilities/SystemCapability.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Shared/ICommandRunner.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Shared/LocalCommandRunner.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Shared/Mxc/MxcAvailability.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Shared/Mxc/MxcCommandRunner.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Shared/Mxc/MxcConfig.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Shared/Mxc/MxcConfigBuilder.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Shared/Mxc/MxcPolicyBuilder.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Shared/Mxc/SandboxPolicy.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI.csproj`
- `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Pages/SandboxPage.xaml.cs`
- `src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/Services/NodeService.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/Mxc/Golden/sdk-config-*.json`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/Mxc/MxcAvailabilityTests.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/Mxc/MxcCommandRunnerIntegrationTests.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/Mxc/MxcCommandRunnerTests.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/Mxc/MxcConfigBuilderTests.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/Mxc/MxcPolicyBuilderTests.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Shared.Tests/SystemRunTests.cs`
- `tests/OpenClaw.Tray.Tests/InstallerIssAssertionTests.cs`

## Validation

Local validation on Windows Release Preview 25H2 build `26200.8728`, Portuguese Windows locale:

- `./build.ps1`: passed, all builds succeeded after the `isolation_session` gate fix.
- Focused `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` filter `MxcAvailabilityTests`: passed, `19` total, `0` failed.
- Focused `OpenClaw.Shared.Tests` filter `MxcAvailabilityTests|MxcConfigBuilderTests|MxcPolicyBuilderTests|MxcCommandRunnerTests|DirectAppContainerExecutorTests`: passed, `90` total, `0` failed.
- `dotnet build ./src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI.csproj -c Debug -r win-x64 --no-restore --verbosity minimal`: passed, `0` warnings, `0` errors.
- Focused final secure-contract filter `MxcCommandRunnerTests|MxcCommandRunnerIntegrationTests|MxcConfigBuilderTests|MxcPolicyBuilderTests|LocalCommandRunnerTests|SystemRunTests|ExecApprovalPolicyTests`: passed, `148` passed, `4` skipped, `0` failed.
- `dotnet build ./src/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI/OpenClaw.Tray.WinUI.csproj --no-restore -p:Platform=x64`: passed, `0` warnings, `0` errors.
- autoreview first pass: found one accepted P2 issue where `isolation_session` reused the processcontainer build table.
- Fix applied: split `ProcessContainerSupportedBuilds` from `IsolationSessionSupportedBuilds`, with `isolation_session` gated to `26300.8553+`.
- Later autoreview pass after the fail-closed/PATH grant update: found one accepted P1 issue where shell PATH bootstrap no longer granted backend-safe PATH tools.
- Fix applied: re-added backend-safe readonly PATH grants and added coverage that protected PATH dirs remain PATH-only.
- Later autoreview pass found one accepted P1 issue where omitted-shell approval still evaluated as `powershell` while host execution could resolve to `pwsh`.
- Fix applied: runners now expose an effective shell for approval; host opt-out can approve `pwsh`, while sandboxed MXC omitted-shell execution remains `powershell`.
- Final autoreview rerun: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Runtime Proof Status

Local runtime proof completed on the current stacked PR2 checkout:

- Direct MXC Gate 1/2 on build `26200.8728`: `wxc-exec --probe` reported `tier=appcontainer-dacl`; positive temp write passed; denied-read smoke did not leak denied content; temp root was removed.
- Isolated Companion MCP-only preflight from patched build output passed using isolated data dirs.
- Temporary narrow MCP `system.run` smoke passed with command `echo OPENCLAW_SYSTEM_RUN_MXC_OK` and returned the expected stdout marker.
- The helper's pass condition validates log evidence for `[mxc] availability`, `executor=mxc-direct-appc`, `contained=True`, config `version=0.7.0-alpha`, `envKeys=[]`, `containment=mxc`, and absence of `UNCONTAINED`.
- Fail-closed sandbox-unavailable behavior is test-proven: with sandbox ON, unavailable MXC/runtime loss returns a typed deny and does not invoke the host fallback; with sandbox OFF, host execution is explicit.

Remaining publication limits:

- Full Gateway-mode proof remains optional and is not claimed here.
- Installed Companion replacement/profile migration proof remains unclaimed.
- Broad full-suite green status remains unclaimed.

Planned PR3:

- Run the broader Gateway-mode validation path safely.
- Validate normal node/Companion behavior beyond isolated MCP-only preflight.
- Validate installed Companion replacement/profile migration only if needed and without folding that risk into this PR.
- Land any resulting fixes separately instead of expanding this PR's scope.

Not claimed:

- Full Gateway-mode Companion pairing/runtime proof.
- Installed Companion replacement or profile migration proof.
- End-to-end product proof.

## Notes

This PR does not modify installed Companion state, Gateway pairing, WSL Gateway setup, command allowlists, or permanent `system.run` exposure. The Companion proof used local build output and isolated data directories.

This PR intentionally separates shell behavior by containment boundary: uncontained host execution prefers `pwsh` when available, while MXC processcontainer defaults to Windows PowerShell 5.1 until PowerShell 7 can start without requiring an unsafe root-drive grant on the AppContainer-DACL tier.

Broad-suite note: this local split validation does not claim the whole suite is green. Broad runs in this environment hit existing `%APPDATA%`/ACL/`HttpListener` environment failures outside this PR's changed surfaces.
